---
title: "Teaching & Mentoring"
page-layout: full
---

## Classroom Teaching

### Instructor of Record

**Promoting change through social psychology** — MA seminar, University of Oslo (Global Mobility, Inclusion, and Diversity in Society program)

**The social psychology of inequality** — BA seminar, Aarhus University Department of Psychology [median instructor evaluation: 4.9/5.0]

**Separating fact from fiction in psychological science** — First-year BA writing-intensive seminar developed at the University of Michigan's Sweetland Center for Writing. [[Syllabus](https://drive.google.com/)] [[Assignments](https://drive.google.com/)] [Fall 2021; median evaluation: 4.9/5.0]

---

### Graduate Student Instructor

**Research Methods in Psychology** (Lab instructor)

- Winter 2020: median 5.0/5.0
- Fall 2020: median 4.9/5.0

Co-created three critical pedagogy modules with Nadia Vossoughi; presented at the Society for Personality and Social Psychology conference (2021); received the Provost's Teaching Innovation Prize. [[Modules (OSF)](https://osf.io/)] [[SPSP presentation (OSF)](https://osf.io/)]

**Introduction to Social Psychology** (Section instructor)

- Winter 2019: median 4.9/5.0
- Fall 2019: median 4.9/5.0

**Introduction to Psychology** (Grader)

---

## Research Mentoring

### Independent Research Advising

- **Ariella Kushner** (Winter 2021 & 2022): Infectious disease concealment research; honors thesis co-advised with Dr. Josh Ackerman — "To sneeze or not to sneeze: The interpersonal consequences of infectious disease concealment"
- **William Gonzalez** (Winter 2022): "Lay beliefs about scent and social class"

### Lab-Based Research Advising

**Evolutionary Social Psychology Lab** (Dr. Josh Ackerman, University of Michigan):
Haley Henry, Cara Jhang, Jian Sun, Tessa Woryk, Caitlin Hoyng, Julia Salamango (2019); Ariella Kushner, Emmaleigh Krause, Lily Jankowiak (2020)

**Mind in Society Lab** (Dr. Nick Camp, University of Michigan):
Lee Hickman, Samantha Cole, Yi (Bella) Jin, Manjiang Shen (2022)

---

## Other Mentoring

**Graduate Student Instructional Consultant** — Center for Research on Learning and Teaching, University of Michigan (classroom observation and coaching for graduate student instructors)

**Academic Mentor** — Academic Success Program, University of Michigan Athletics (monitored academic progress for varsity football players)
